mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly measures consumer spending strength; higher spending lifts near-term corporate revenue expectations, causing institutional investors to bid up equity prices and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between RSAFS monthly changes and S&P 500 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03, or the mean return in the top quintile of RSAFS changes is not statistically greater than the bottom quintile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7.5% signals severe labor market slack that forces the central bank to maintain accommodative policy for an extended period; this sustained easing expectation compresses the equity discount rate and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observed months when UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, or the average return conditional on this trigger is below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of low consumer price inflation and a near-zero federal funds rate produces a strongly negative real policy rate; this joint condition compresses real discount rates and spurs yield-seeking portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the effect of either variable alone.
failure_condition: A regression of 20-day S&P 500 returns on the interaction term CPIAUCSL*FEDFUNDS yields a t-statistic below 1.5, or the interaction adds less than 0.04 incremental Rank IC over the main effects in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when the unemployment rate is above 6%, because in this regime supply-side expansion reflects accelerating demand rather than overheating; when below 6%, the same growth signals inflation risk and valuation compression.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6%, month-over-month INDPRO growth exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 against 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the mean forward return conditional on positive INDPRO growth is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP level surprises, captured by GDPC1, drive systematic revisions to consensus earnings estimates; positive GDP realizations lead to upward earnings revisions that lift equity prices over the subsequent 60 trading days as the information diffuses across the market.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 changes against 60-day S&P 500 forward returns is below 0.02, or the top quintile of GDP surprises does not outperform the bottom quintile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,000 thousand units signal a contraction in residential construction and household formation that reduces demand for building materials, durable goods, and credit; this demand shortfall compresses cyclical earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1,000, or the average return conditional on this trigger exceeds the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales capture a self-reinforcing demand-employment feedback loop; rising payrolls boost disposable income and consumption, while rising sales validate hiring, jointly lifting earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal using PAYEMS and RSAFS produces a lower walk-forward Rank IC than either univariate signal alone over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns only when the unemployment rate exceeds 7%; outside this regime the effect disappears because the signal is dominated by cyclical growth expectations rather than monetary accommodation.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and UNRATE exceeds 7%, S&P 500 5-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average conditional return is below zero.
